items.title: USDA opens $5 million SNAP Fraud Framework Implementation Grants for states (apply by July 17, 2026)
items.summary: USDA announced up to $5 million in FY26 SNAP Fraud Framework Implementation Grants to help states upgrade data analytics and fraud detection tools, strengthen oversight, and expand SNAP integrity training. The application window runs through July 17, 2026.

items.title: USDA updates Privacy Act notice for the National SNAP Information Database after 450+ public comments
items.summary: USDA published a modified Privacy Act System of Records Notice for USDA/FNA-15 (the National SNAP Information Database), which consolidates state SNAP participant data for eligibility verification and program integrity. The updated notice ties disclosures to what the Food and Nutrition Act permits and reflects changes after more than 450 public comments.

items.title: MACPAC: Medicaid community engagement requirements start Jan. 1, 2027; notices due by June 30, 2026
items.summary: MACPAC reports that Medicaid community engagement requirements begin January 1, 2027, and states must notify affected people at least 3 months before the look-back period preceding Dec. 31, 2026 (which implies notices by June 30, 2026). MACPAC cites a Congressional Budget Office estimate of 5.3 million fewer people insured in 2034 and $326 billion in savings over the period.
items.source: Medicaid and CHIP Payment and Access Commission (MACPAC)

items.title: CMS guidance: new Medicaid/CHIP federal funding limits by immigration status take effect Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS guidance says that beginning October 1, 2026, federal financial participation (FFP) for non-emergency Medicaid and certain CHIP coverage is generally limited to U.S. citizens/nationals and specified eligible noncitizen groups (including lawful permanent residents, Cuban/Haitian entrants, and COFA migrants), with limited exceptions. States must redetermine potentially affected current beneficiaries by Oct. 1, 2026, and may provide a 90-day reasonable opportunity period when verification cannot be promptly completed.

items.title: IRS: child and dependent care credit maximum rate increases to 50% for 2026 (with $3,000/$6,000 expense limits)
items.summary: IRS guidance notes that for 2026, legislation enhanced the child and dependent care credit: the qualifying expense limits remain $3,000 (or $6,000 for two or more qualifying children), but the maximum credit rate increases from 35% to 50%. This change can increase the maximum credit for eligible families with child care expenses.

items.title: IRS: up to $5,120 of the adoption credit is refundable for 2026; max credit/exclusion rises to $17,670
items.summary: IRS guidance states that for 2026, up to $5,120 of the adoption credit is refundable (calculated per child). The maximum adoption credit or exclusion for employer-provided adoption benefits increases to $17,670, and eligibility requires modified AGI under $305,080.

items.title: IRS: new 2026 Child Tax Credit/Additional CTC rule requires a valid SSN for employment (issued by filing deadline)
items.summary: IRS guidance says that beginning in 2026, to claim the Child Tax Credit (CTC) or Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC), the filer must have a valid Social Security number for employment issued before the return due date (including extensions). On joint returns, only one spouse must have a valid SSN for employment, while the other spouse must have an SSN or ITIN issued by the filing due date.

items.title: USDA sets WIC income guidelines for July 1, 2026–June 30, 2027 (example: family of 4 up to $61,050 at 185% FPL)
items.summary: USDA published updated WIC income eligibility guidelines effective July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027. For the 48 contiguous states and D.C., the 185% guideline is $29,526 annually for a 1-person household and $61,050 annually for a 4-person household.

items.title: Education Department: Workforce Pell and new Pell ineligibility provisions take effect July 1, 2026
items.summary: Federal Student Aid posted an announcement stating Workforce Pell begins July 1, 2026. The same announcement says new Federal Pell Grant ineligibility provisions also take effect July 1, 2026.
items.source: Federal Student Aid (U.S. Department of Education)
